narration: You pay 22% income tax on everything you earn. America's 400 wealthiest paid an average of 8.2%. Here's the exact legal strategy making that possible.
visual: Dark background. Split screen: casual worker silhouette on left with '22%' in red animating up from zero, suited wealthy figure on right with '8.2%' in green animating up. Text at bottom: 'SAME COUNTRY. DIFFERENT RULES.'

narration: It's called buy, borrow, die. They buy appreciating assets, never sell them, then borrow cash against the portfolio instead. No sale means zero taxable income.
visual: Light background. Three large circles spring into view in sequence: stock chart icon labeled 'BUY', bank building labeled 'BORROW', gravestone labeled 'DIE'. Connecting arrows animate between them. Bottom text: 'NO SALE = NO TAX'

narration: The math is staggering. On $100 million, a salary means $37 million in taxes. But borrow $50 million against your portfolio and the interest is only $2 million. That's $35 million saved.
visual: Dark background. Left column: money bag with '$37M TAX' in red. Right column: bank building with '$2M INTEREST' in blue. Large 'VS' between them. Bottom: 'SAVES $35,000,000' in green animating in.

narration: ProPublica proved it with leaked IRS data. The 400 richest Americans paid an effective rate of 8.2% — less than a nurse, less than a teacher, less than you.
visual: Light background. Two vertical bars growing upward: left bar labeled 'YOU' reaches 22% in red, right bar labeled 'TOP 400' stops at 8.2% in blue. Percentage numbers animate up as bars grow.

narration: And here's the final trap door. When they die, all unrealized gains get a step-up in basis — every lifetime of gains wiped clean. Their heirs inherit millions completely tax-free.
visual: Dark background. Left-to-right timeline: person holding stock at $1M, chart growing to $100M, gravestone icon, reset arrow labeled 'STEP-UP', heir figure receiving '$100M'. Text: 'ZERO TAX OWED. FOREVER.'

narration: You can't fully copy this, but you can think like them. Max your Roth IRA, harvest tax losses, and hold appreciating assets long-term. Realize gains on your schedule, not the IRS's.
visual: Light background. Three tips spring in sequentially: Roth IRA jar labeled 'MAX ROTH IRA', downward chart labeled 'HARVEST LOSSES', house icon labeled 'HOLD ASSETS'. Bottom: 'FOLLOW FOR MORE' in blue.
